name: Smithery
type: MCP server deployment and hosting platform
authentication: Requires Smithery API key (generated from Smithery dashboard). Set via SMITHERY_API_KEY env var.
notes: No direct web API for submissions. Deployment is done via their CLI tool which handles building and publishing. Servers are also auto-indexed from public GitHub repositories.

name: MCP.so

required_fields.name: Name of the MCP server or client (text input)
required_fields.url: GitHub URL of the MCP server (URL input, placeholder: https://github.com/chatmcp/mcp-server-github)

authentication: None required for web form submission
notes: Publicly accessible submission form. No API key needed. Form requires server name and GitHub repository URL. Also auto-indexes from public GitHub repositories.

name: Glama
type: MCP server registry with hosting and gateway
authentication: Requires account (paid, $9-$80/month). API access requires API key for gateway/proxy features.
rate_limits: Pro plan: No rate limits on AI credits. 100k logs/month (Starter). Different tiers available.
notes: Glama is a superset of the official MCP Registry. It automatically scans and indexes open-source MCP servers from GitHub. No manual submission API exists. Once indexed, users can host servers on Glama's platform (paid). Servers are scored for quality, license, and maintenance.

name: MCP Get (MCP Package Registry)
authentication: None required for GET (public read API). Submission via web form likely requires no auth.
notes: Public REST API at /api/packages returns JSON array of ~16,000 packages. Each package has id, name, description, vendor, sourceUrl, homepage, license, runtime, readme, and usage stats. Also has a web submission form at /submit. The API is read-only (GET only).

name: OpenTools
type: AI tools directory (includes MCP servers section)
required_fields: Not determinable from server-side rendering (client-rendered form). Likely includes tool name, description, URL, category.
authentication: None required for web form submission
notes: Submit page at /friends/launch-tool. Also has a dedicated MCP server directory at /mcp. The site has 2,500+ AI tools listed including MCP servers. Submission form is client-side rendered (Next.js). No public REST API found. 782K+ monthly visitors.
